user_query:
I need a fully time-blocked, energy-aware daily plan for 2026-04-15. Please read my inputs from input/tasks.csv, input/commitments.csv, and input/preferences.yaml, then write two artifacts: (1) output/plan.md using the exact headings and structure of a research-backed daily plan (Top 3 priorities, hour-by-hour schedule, success criteria, and evening check-in), and (2) output/schedule.json summarizing the computed top priorities and the final block-by-block schedule.

Instructions and rules:
1) Parse tasks and compute a score for each task = impact × urgency. Break ties by higher impact, then by shorter effort_minutes. Select the Top 3.
2) Respect input/preferences.yaml for date, working hours, lunch, and energy windows. Treat fixed commitments in input/commitments.csv as immovable.
3) Sequencing constraints (follow exactly if they don’t conflict with fixed commitments):
   - Morning Prime: 08:00–09:00 (routine, not counted as work time)
   - Priority #1 block: 09:00–11:00 (deep work, in the morning peak)
   - Break: 11:00–11:15
   - Lunch: 12:30–13:30 (recovery)
   - Priority #3 block: 13:30–15:00 (focused work, pre-meeting)
   - Break: 15:00–15:15
   - Insert the fixed meeting from commitments exactly at its given time
   - Priority #2 block: 15:45–16:45 (or the next immediately available slot after the meeting, matching these times for this data)
   - Admin & Communication: 16:45–17:30 (late afternoon, low-energy)
   - Planning & Wrap-Up: 17:30–18:00
4) Only schedule up to 80% of the total working hours as actual work/meetings (exclude routine and breaks from this calculation). Keep breaks as listed above.
5) Place the #1 priority in the morning peak; put #2 in the secondary peak or the next available slot after the meeting; #3 in remaining focused time as specified. Keep the meeting at the exact time from input/commitments.csv.
6) In output/plan.md, include these sections and headings verbatim: "Daily Plan - ...", "Today's Mission", "Top 3 Priorities", "Time-Blocked Schedule", "Success Criteria", and "Evening Check-In". Make the Top 3 items specific and measurable, and list the meeting by name at its scheduled time in the timetable.
7) Also create output/schedule.json with this structure:
   {
     "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
     "top_priorities": [ {"name": str, "score": int, "rank": 1|2|3}, ... ],
     "blocks": [
       {"start": "HH:MM", "end": "HH:MM", "label": str, "type": "routine|work|break|meeting", "priority": 1|2|3|null}
     ]
   }
   - Use 24-hour times. Label the meeting block exactly with its title from input/commitments.csv. Mark Morning Prime as type "routine". Mark lunch and the two short rests as type "break".

Write the final files to output/plan.md and output/schedule.json. Do not write anywhere else.
